passive_fishing.best_stand_spot: Stillwater Village (shore-based 4-Rod Stand) — primary base for the Club Rod Oasis
passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Calm waters, deep-enough drop-offs for carp and bream, easy walking distance to the active spinning zone. Mid-lake feeder casts (30-50 ft) cover the productive carp shelf without requiring boat. Multiple bait types can be cycled across the 4 rods to target Common/Mirror Carp + Bream + Tench + Wels Cat simultaneously.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Cast Pike/Zander spinnerbaits along the dock/structure 50-150 ft to either side of the rod stand. Stay within 100 ft of the rods so you can hear/see strike alerts and reach a bite within 5 seconds. Fan-cast sweeping arcs without crossing your stand rod lines.
strategy: Drop the 4-Rod Stand at Stillwater Village shoreline facing deeper water
Slot 1 (Mega Chuber + Banana Boilies) cast 50-80 ft to the carp shelf
Slot 2 (Mokonzi Method Feeder + Corn) cast 30-50 ft for Bream/Tench/mixed Carp attraction
Slot 3 (Tribal Totem + Large Cutbait) cast 60-100 ft to deeper water for Wels Catfish
Slot 4 (Heavy Chaser + alt-flavor Boilies/Pellet) cast 40-70 ft as the carp diversifier
Slot 5 (Salmonster + Spinnerbait) IN HAND — fan-cast Pike/Zander structure 50-150 ft from stand position
Recast/refresh stand rods every 10-15 min — degraded bait stops attracting carp
When carp keepnet fills, sell at travel screen and reset all 4 stand rods
income_estimate: Common/Mirror Carp passive: 2-3 fish/hour at avg 8-12 lb = ~$3,000-$5,000/hr per active stand rod
Wels Catfish on Tribal Totem: high-variance — 1 catch can be $2,000-$10,000+ depending on size
Method Feeder Bream/Tench bonus: $500-$1,500/hr fill-in income
Spinnerbait Pike/Zander active: $1,000-$3,000/hr bonus while stand soaks
Total realistic target: $7,000-$15,000/hr combined active+passive at Stillwater Village

boat_trolling.boat_required: GoldenAlbatross Adventure Kayak or Garry Scott Draco Bass Boat (Advanced License required for boat fishing)
boat_trolling.summary: Boat trolling at Sander Baggersee is HIGHLY VIABLE for Pike, Zander, and Wels Catfish along submerged structure. Lake-in-a-Lake area is the prime trolling zone — accessible only by boat and holds the largest predators.
boat_trolling.staging_spot: Lake in a Lake (boat-only inner water body)
boat_trolling.staging_why: Inner lake structure holds the deepest pools and largest Wels Cats + trophy Pike. No shore competition. Drift along the connecting channels for big predator strikes.
boat_trolling.route: Launch at Southern Docks → Troll east toward Lake-in-a-Lake → Slow troll (2-3 mph) along the channel and around the inner lake perimeter → Anchor at deeper holes for stationary cat fishing
target_fish: Wels Catfish (trophy/unique - main reason to troll)
recommended_lures: Spinnerbait 1 Oz #4/0 with Titanium leader — Pike/Zander all-purpose troll
Jerkbait Medium #2/0 — slow troll for Pike along weed edges
Crankbait 6-8 ft #4/0 deep diver — covers mid-water column for big Pike + Zander
Soft Plastic Shad 4-6" on JigHead 3/4-1 Oz — bottom-bouncing Zander rig
Live Small Minnows under bobber — dead-drift behind boat for Pike (most natural)
notes: Slow troll speed: 2-3 mph — Pike and Zander prefer slower presentations than salmon
TITANIUM LEADERS MANDATORY — Pike WILL be caught and will bite off Fluoro
Two-rod troll spread: one shallow (Spinnerbait), one deep (Crankbait) covers the column
Drop anchor at Lake-in-a-Lake holes for stationary Wels Catfish bottom rigs (Tribal Totem + Cutbait)
Watch fish finder — Wels Catfish show as massive single returns near bottom in deep holes
Best trolling windows: dawn (5-8am in-game) and dusk (6-9pm) when predators are most active
Boat travel from Southern Docks → Lake-in-a-Lake takes ~5-10 min in-game depending on boat speed

location.name: Sander Baggersee Lake
location.level_note: DLC-unlocked early at Level 38. Native level 42. First visit 2026-04-14.

advanced_germany.recommendation: STRONGLY RECOMMENDED. Boat access opens up Lake-in-a-Lake and deeper carp/cat zones. Night fishing for Eel/Wels Cat. 1100 BC unlimited is the best long-term value.

primary_income_targets: Bighead Carp - filter feeder, big trophy class
Silver Carp - filter feeder, similar to Bighead
Grass Carp - plant-eater, very large class
Common Carp / Mirror Carp / Leather Carp - traditional carp targets
fish_summary.monster_fish: Monster ID 10964 (look up in-game - likely a Bighead/Silver Carp variant)

spots.name: Bavarian Woods
spots.description: Wooded shoreline - likely shore bottom for carp

spots.name: Fields of Gold
spots.description: Open area - potentially good for boat float fishing

spots.name: Lake in a Lake
spots.description: Inner/secondary water body - BOAT REQUIRED, often a hotspot for big carp/cats

spots.name: Southern Docks
spots.description: Dock structure - good for predator casting + carp bottom

spots.name: Stillwater Village
spots.description: Calm village waters - float fishing primary

loadout_strategy.philosophy: CARP-FOCUSED REVISION from Kaniq salmon setup. Sander Baggersee is primarily a CARP LAKE with predator bonuses. Convert most active-spoon rods to carp/feeder/match setups. Keep 1-2 active rods for Pike/Zander predator targeting (TITANIUM LEADER REQUIRED for pike). Mega Chuber 12'4" SE is the carp specialist rod - deploy with confidence here.
key_changes_from_kaniq: Drop Medium Spoon 6/7 Oz salmon rigs - swap to carp boilie/corn/feeder setups
Deploy Mega Chuber 12'4" SE as primary carp specialist rod (Banana Boilies on hair rig)
Convert active rods to spinnerbait/jerkbait for Pike/Zander predator coverage
Multiple feeder rods with Method Mix groundbait for Bream/Tench/Carp attraction
Keep Tribal Totem + Mokonzi for Wels Catfish on Cutbait (cross-location proven)
Add Hair Rig + Boilie #1/0-#2/0 to terminal tackle inventory

slots.rod: Mega Chuber 12'4" SE (Specialist Carp Rod, X-Heavy)
slots.terminal_tackle: Carp Sinker 4 1/16 Oz, Hair Rig + Hook #2/0
slots.bait: Banana Sinking Boilies (or Strawberry/Tutti Frutti boilie)
slots.notes: PRIMARY CARP ROD - confirmed working at SJD (Common Carp 8.186 lb on Banana Boilies). Sander Baggersee is the natural home for this setup. Hair rig + boilie is the German carp standard. Cast to deeper water 30-50 ft from shore.

slots.terminal_tackle: Open Method Feeder 4 1/2 Oz + Mix Grand Fish Feeder, Hook #6
slots.notes: Method feeder + groundbait = dynamite for German carp lake. Corn on small hook attracts ALL carp species + Bream + Tench. Mix Grand Fish Feeder concentrates the spread. NO TITANIUM (no pike on bottom drift).

Wels Catfish at Sander Baggersee can grow to 100+ lb. Cross-location proven cat killer. Use Large Cutbait + #6/0 (downsize from #4/0 if too aggressive). Switch to Worm or Eel Cutbait at night for European Eel. CAUTION: Wels Cats here are larger than Saint-Croix Blue Cats.

slots.bait: Boilies (alt flavor) or Halibut Pellets or Tiger Nuts
slots.notes: Second carp rod with alternate boilie flavor or pellet. Diversifies bait coverage on the 4-rod stand. Useful for Bighead Carp competition - try Method Mix + small pellet hookbait.
Predator rod with Titanium leader for pike bite-off. Spinnerbait works for both Pike and Zander. Try around dock structure (Southern Docks) and weed edges (Bavarian Woods).
Jighead + soft plastic = standard Zander rig. Drop-and-pause technique works. Try drop-offs from dock structure. Long range to reach deeper water.

Knock out the 7 panfish/Euro species for first-catch XP bonuses. Same telescopic that worked at Tiber and Lesni Vila. Switch baits per target species. Light line is fine - separated from carp/cat zones.

baits_to_buy: Banana Sinking Boilies (already own - confirmed at SJD)

highest_value_targets: Wels Catfish - massive size potential = highest single-fish payoff
Bighead Carp - filter feeder, large trophy class, competition target
Grass Carp - very large size, carp pricing tier
Common Carp - reliable income, easy to catch with proper setup
session_plan: 1. Buy Advanced Germany License unlimited (1100 BC) for boat + night fishing
3. Spot 1: Stillwater Village or Bavarian Woods - deploy 4-Rod Stand carp setup (slots 1-4) + active rod 5
4. Boat to Lake-in-a-Lake mid-day for big carp/cat zones (if Advanced License)
5. Switch to telescopic (slot 7) at calm panfish spot for species completion
6. Night session: Tribal Totem with Worm/Eel bait for European Eel + Wels Cat
7. Return to dock for active Zander/Pike with slots 5+6
income_strategy.expected_income_target: 30,000-60,000 credits / session (depends on Wels Cat hits + Bighead competition success)

bighead_carp_competition_strategy.overview: Bighead Carp competition this afternoon. Bighead are FILTER FEEDERS - they suspend in mid-water filtering plankton. Standard bottom carp rigs may not work optimally.
preparation: Stock up on Halibut Pellets and Method Mix (filter-feeder magnets)
Use Method Feeder (slot 2 or 4) with HEAVY groundbait to create suspended cloud in mid-water
Try smaller hooks (#6 - #4) with single pellet or fake/buoyant boilie hookbait
Set hookbait to suspend 6-12 inches off the bottom (PVA bag or pop-up presentation)
Bighead respond to Pellets, Method Mix, Bread, Tutti Frutti boilies
Position over deeper water near drop-offs (boat or long-cast from shore)

rig_setup.rod: Slot 2 (Mokonzi feeder) or Slot 4 (Heavy Chaser carp)
rig_setup.feeder: Open Method Feeder 4 1/2 Oz packed with Method Mix
rig_setup.hook: Carp Hook #4 to #6 with hair rig
rig_setup.hookbait: Single Halibut Pellet OR Pop-up Boilie 12-15mm
rig_setup.presentation: Hookbait suspended 4-8 inches above feeder via stiff hair rig

competition_tips: ARRIVE EARLY - reel spots are limited, get a good shoreline position
PRE-BAIT the spot - cast 3-5 method feeders without hook to build groundbait pile before deploying real rod
RECAST every 15-20 min to refresh the groundbait
Bighead are SCHOOL FISH - one catch usually means more nearby, stay put
Watch for surface bubbles/movements - sign of active feeding school
bighead_carp_competition_strategy.fallback_if_struggling: If Bighead aren't responding, switch to slot 1 (Mega Chuber + Banana Boilies) for Common/Mirror Carp - still scores in the carp family if competition allows

missions_to_check: Sander Baggersee Exploration I (catch listed species - 17 species available)
Bighead Carp Master missions (relevant for competition)
critical_warnings: NORTHERN PIKE PRESENT - active rods (slots 5, 6) MUST use Titanium leader
WELS CATFISH potentially MASSIVE (100+ lb) - ensure Tribal Totem + heaviest line/leader on slot 3
Boat fishing requires Advanced License - budget 1100 BC unlimited or 8000 credits/day
Sander Baggersee is a CARP-FIRST location - don't bring salmon/sturgeon mindset
Some carp species (Bighead, Silver) are filter feeders - need pellets/groundbait, NOT just boilies

loadout_progress.improvement_summary: Carp-focused 7-slot for first Sander Baggersee trip. Mega Chuber + Banana Boilies confirmed cross-location. Mokonzi feeder repurposed for carp/bream. Tribal Totem keeps cat duty (now Wels). Active rods reduced to 2 (Pike/Zander). Telescopic for panfish completion. CRITICAL: Titanium leaders on all active rods for pike bite-off.

user_goals: Maximize income (3x Saint-Croix-style hourly rate target)
Maximize XP (first-catch bonuses on 17 new species)
Bighead Carp competition this afternoon (preparation included above)
